mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated, markets infer restrictive policy will persist and terminal-rate risk stays high, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days as growth-sensitive equity valuation pressure rises. The effect fails if inflation softens sharply enough that rate-cut expectations dominate the same window.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.0%, or if the sign flips persistently after controlling for inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL acceleration above its recent trend triggers a repricing toward tighter policy and higher real discount rates, which depresses equities over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when CPI accelerates but FEDFUNDS is already falling, because easing expectations offset the inflation shock.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month average by 0.5pp
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE combined with a slowing PAYEMS trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker earnings growth, leading to negative SPX performance over the next 5 trading days as investors de-risk quickly. The mechanism fails if unemployment rises because of temporary labor supply effects while payroll growth remains strong.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS 3-month change turns negative
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint trigger is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if PAYEMS remains positive and the signal still predicts weakness.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal demand and revenue momentum, supporting equity prices over a 20-day horizon as firms beat near-term earnings expectations. The effect fails when demand strength is offset by sharply rising rates or deteriorating labor conditions.
failure_condition: Rejected if high RSAFS growth periods do not outperform baseline over 20 trading days, or if the sign becomes negative after repeated revisions are excluded.

mechanism_narrative: A continued rise in INDPRO together with higher HOUST marks a supply-side expansion that lowers inflation pressure and supports risk assets over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if production rises only because of inventory rebuilding while housing activity remains weak.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint expansion signal is followed by negative 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if INDPRO rises without concurrent HOUST improvement.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating faster than output can absorb, which compresses earnings expectations and drives SPX lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if payroll weakness is temporary and unemployment does not confirm a broader slowdown.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change turns negative while UNRATE increases by at least 0.1pp over the same span
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered cases produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE is flat.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in GDPC1 growth after a prior expansion phase signals weaker final demand and slower corporate revenue growth, which lowers equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect fails if GDP slows but consumer spending and payrolls remain strong enough to offset the deceleration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Rejected if GDP slowdowns are followed by positive 60-day returns more than half the time, or if the effect vanishes once retail sales and payrolls are included.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of stable CPIAUCSL and a flat-to-falling FEDFUNDS rate implies disinflation plus easing policy, which compresses real yields and supports equities over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism fails if inflation stability coincides with tightening guidance rather than actual or expected cuts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is unchanged or down over 2 months and CPIAUCSL monthly change stays below 0.3%
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint disinflation-easing condition fails to deliver positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign turns negative when both series are stable.
